title: Iterative maps emerging from cohomological structure of primes
abstract: Prime numbers appeared in contexts spanning statistical mechanics, quantum mechanics and dynamical systems. However, the mechanisms governing the irregularities observed in their sequence and linking them to physical systems remained unclear. Here, it is shown that prime gaps at different separation distances follow a function depending on that distance and can be described by an iterative map which predicts the primary growth of successive primes. On the other hand, the analysis of remaining fluctuations reveals the existence of a well-defined cohomological structure, where the deterministic functional relation holds for primes up to small decaying fluctuations. In consequence, the long-range correlations as well as local jumps in primes encode the underlying cohomological structure where prime numbers are states of a given system that becomes deterministic asymptotically. Remarkably, the solution to this cohomological equation turns out to be the logarithmic integral function.
summary: This paper proposes that the distribution of prime numbers is not purely random but follows a deterministic iterative map and a cohomological structure. By treating primes as states in a dynamical system, the author shows that the logarithmic integral function emerges as a solution to the governing evolution equation. This provides a new way to link number theory to the statistical mechanics of complex, correlated systems.
main_problem: The paper seeks to understand the underlying mechanisms governing the irregularities in the sequence of prime numbers and determine if their distribution exhibits a deterministic structure linked to physical systems.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that prime gaps follow an iterative map and that the fluctuations around this trend are governed by a well-defined cohomological structure, with the logarithmic integral function serving as a solution.
method: The study uses statistical analysis of tau-gaps, residual analysis (subtractive and relative), and fitting prime distributions to Gamma distributions, alongside asymptotic analysis of a proposed cohomological equation.
model_or_system: The prime number sequence is modeled as a dynamical system exhibiting long-range correlations, where primes are treated as states of a system governed by a non-stationary, parametrically driven process and a cohomological equation.
key_observables: tau-gaps (d_tau), relative residuals (r), subtractive residuals (s), and the error term (epsilon).
important_parameters: gap distance (tau), prime index (n), shape parameter (alpha), and the decay rate (lambda).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the prime sequence can be treated as a system with a smooth backbone and that the asymptotic behavior of the error term can be modeled via the Mean Value Theorem and Stirling's formula.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows tau-gaps and their fit to an iterative map; Fig 2 displays residuals and their variance; Fig 3 compares prime distributions to random number distributions; Fig 4 compares real gaps to model values; Fig 6 compares the prime counting function to Li(x) and the proposed model solution.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of prime irregularities, presents a model using an iterative map and cohomological equation, performs statistical validation using gap analysis and residual distributions, and concludes by connecting the framework to the logarithmic integral function.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
